body_txt: A character is subjected to a Long List of their sins and transgressions, often impossibly long. The list may be presented in person, or written out (perhaps on a "Wanted!" Poster ). Alternatively, a character is placed in purgatory or otherwise judged for their crimes, with a convenient long list of crimes at hand, possibly because the Powers That Be have Surveillance as the Plot Demands . If the character is a Lovable Rogue , Trickster , or similarly sly trope, expect them to quip, quibble, or comically protest as the sins are read that these deeds are being misrepresented or taken out of context; this is always immediately followed by the accuser stating an especially heinous sin, to which the accused character will admit they don't really have a defense for that one. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking (or Bread, Eggs, Milk, Squick , if you're delving into Black Comedy ) is typically invoked, making this a sister trope. This can also invoke a Dissimile if the defense or accuser remarks that the target's record is pretty clean aside from the list. On the other hand, it can be subverted if it becomes Arson, Murder, and Lifesaving . The details on the list may qualify as Noodle Incidents . The reading of the list is a popular introduction to a Boxed Crook plot, to emphasize how much trouble the protagonist is in if they don't comply with their mission. Can lead to Longer-Than-Life Sentence .
